policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-014, CS-RULE-015, FIN-RULE-001, FIN-RULE-003, FIN-RULE-004, FIN-RULE-005, FIN-RULE-010, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
kb_refs: [Section 3.3 - Compensation Matrix, Section 5.6 - ETF Waiver Process]
expected_outcome: Customer is locked into a 24-month contract. Hardwired ethernet test shows speeds at 38% of advertised plan. Hannah verifies the slow-speed evidence per FIN-RULE-003 (hardwired ethernet), then offers two policy-compliant outcomes per the FIN compensation matrix: retroactive downgrade OR ETF-waived contract exit. Customer chooses ETF-waived exit. Because the ETF waiver is an out-of-policy override requiring Manager Approval, Hannah opens a Manager Approval ticket per FIN-RULE-005 instead of promising the waiver outright. PASS — full SOP.

coverage.element: Hardwired ethernet test guided on call
FIN-RULE-001 cat. 3 eligibility (< 50% of plan verified)
Does NOT promise ETF waiver before authorization
Manager Approval ticket for ETF waiver

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Hannah. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: This is Camille Beaufort. I have had it. I'm paying for a three hundred megabit plan, the speeds are nowhere near that, and your contract has me locked in for another fourteen months. So I want to know what we are going to do about this.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Ms. Beaufort, I hear you, and being locked into a contract for service that isn't performing is exactly the kind of situation we have specific policy for. Let me work through this with you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about consistently slow speeds on your plan and the contract obligation. I can certainly help you with that. Before I look at the account, I'll need to verify a few details — could you start with the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-four-nine-three, seven-seven-two-eight, six-six.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. And the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Two-zero-eight-nine.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And the full name on the account, exactly as it appears?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Camille Antoinette Beaufort.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're fully verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good. Let's get to it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I can see you're on Home Pro, three hundred megabits, contract started ten months ago. Twenty-four-month term, so fourteen months remaining. Standard early termination fee on that contract is one hundred and twenty dollars if cancelled today. That's the picture.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Right, that's the problem. One twenty for cancelling service that doesn't work.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Completely understand the frustration of that math. Before we talk about exit, I need to verify the speed claim using the policy's accepted method — a hardwired ethernet test. WiFi tests don't count for this kind of decision, no matter what they show, because too many variables sit between the line and the WiFi device.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I know about the ethernet thing. I read your terms when I signed up. I have a cable ready. Tell me what to do.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's perfect — having you ready saves us time. Do you have a laptop or desktop with an ethernet port and the cable already?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. Cable from router to laptop. WiFi is off on the laptop.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Then we are in great shape. Open Speedtest dot net in your browser, hit go, and tell me the download and upload numbers.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Running… okay. One hundred and fourteen down. Six up.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. Let me run it again from my side to confirm — sometimes the first run is unrepresentative.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Run two — one moment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Run two on my side shows one hundred and nine down. Average of the two is one-eleven-and-a-half down on a three hundred megabit plan.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Right. Like I said.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's confirmed. One-eleven on three hundred is just under thirty-eight percent of plan, well below the fifty percent threshold. Under our policy, that meets the eligibility for refund or contract action.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What are my options exactly?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Two policy-compliant options.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Option one — retroactive plan downgrade. We drop you from the three hundred plan down to the one hundred megabit tier, backdated to the date you first reported the issue. That's effectively crediting you the price difference for the months you've been underserved, and going forward you pay the lower price for what you've actually been getting.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hm.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Option two — contract exit without early termination fee. We waive the one twenty ETF and you're free to leave the service entirely. The waiver isn't automatic; it requires Manager Approval because it's outside my single-call authorization, but the policy clearly supports it given the verified evidence.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Either is on the table?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Either is on the table — this is your choice, not mine. The first is appropriate if you actually want the internet and just need fair pricing for what's reaching you. The second is appropriate if you want to leave entirely and find another provider.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Honestly? I want out. I've already been looking at other providers. I'd rather just close this and move.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's a completely reasonable choice given what we just confirmed.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So how does that work?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Here's exactly how I'm going to handle it, and I want to be transparent — I'm not going to promise you a waived ETF before authorization confirms it, because that's a policy I follow precisely so customers aren't told one thing and then surprised.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fair.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: What I am going to do is open a Manager Approval ticket on this call, with the two ethernet test results, the speed-versus-plan ratio, and your stated preference for the contract exit option. Manager Approval will return a decision within one business day. Given that the evidence is verified and within policy, the expected outcome is approval — but the decision is theirs, not mine, and you'll hear it directly from them.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One business day.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: One business day. They'll call the phone number on file. If approved, the ETF is waived, your account closes effective the date you choose, and a prepaid equipment return label is emailed to you the same day. The standard refund of any prepaid balance follows our normal timeline.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What does that timeline look like?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: We have processed the refund today, but depending on your bank it may take one to two billing cycles to appear.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. And if Manager Approval declines?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Then option one — the retroactive downgrade — is still available to you as a fallback, and that one I can do on the call myself. So you're not without a path either way.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Opening the Manager Approval ticket with all the verified evidence attached.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And I want to acknowledge — you came in frustrated, you were right to be, the numbers backed you up, and now there's a clear path. That's the way these calls should go.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: we ran two hardwired ethernet tests and confirmed your line at thirty-eight percent of advertised plan. You chose the contract-exit-with-ETF-waived option, I opened a Manager Approval ticket with all the verified evidence and your stated preference, and you'll receive a decision within one business day by phone. If approved, account closes and equipment returns the same day. If declined, retroactive downgrade remains available. Does that all sound right?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Ms. Beaufort?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, you handled that better than I expected. Thank you, Hannah.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for the kind words. Your case reference number is N-X-L dash one-nine-four-four-eight-two-seven. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — if you have a moment, we'd really appreciate the feedback. Take care.
